title: Hydrogen storage compositions, methods, and uses thereof
abstract: Described herein are compositions and methods capable of reversibly storing hydrogen in a liquid carrier. Also described herein are methods of releasing the stored hydrogen from the liquid carrier. In some aspects, the storage of hydrogen includes capture of CO2 and formation of formate. The release of hydrogen can include dehydrogenation of the formate on the surface of an appropriate catalyst. Also described herein are systems that include the compositions and methods described herein.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: (a) reacting C0 2 with an amine to form an adduct comprising captured CO2; and (b) hydrogenating the captured CO2 in the presence of a hydrogenation solvent to form formates. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising: (c) dehydrogenating the formates with a catalyst to generate Â¾. 3. The method of any of claims 1-2, wherein the amine is selected from the group consisting of: monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, ethane- 1, 2-diamine, 2-amino- 2-methyl-l -propanol, 2-amino-2-methyl-l, 3, -propanediol, 2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-l,3- propanediol, piperazine, piperidine, 1 -methyl piperazine, 2-methyl piperazine, and combinations thereof. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the amine is piperidine. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the adduct formed in step (a) is a formate-pipridine adduct. 6.
